simulants.name: ALRS-1
simulants.notes: materials of basalt characteristics taken from a mine in New South Wales are used

simulants.name: BP-1
simulants.notes: Discovered as waste pile in aggregate quarry

simulants.name: CUG-1A
simulants.notes: Demonstrates comparatively lower softening point and can achieve densification with reduced energy input relative to JSC-2

simulants.name: CUMT-1
simulants.institution: China Univ. of Mining and Tech

simulants.name: DNA-1A
simulants.notes: Mare simulant from Bolsena volcanic ash; developed for ESA D-Shape 3D-printing construction research by Monolite UK Ltd.

simulants.name: FJS-1
simulants.notes: Manufactured from Mt. Fuji basaltic lava

simulants.name: FJS-2
simulants.notes: Contains the same basalt as FJS-1 but with olivine added. Developed for JAXA to represent the chemistry, mineralogy and geotechnical properties of mare basalt lunar regolith.

simulants.name: JLU-H
simulants.notes: Contains coarse particles up to 50 mm

simulants.name: JSC-1AF
simulants.notes: Very fine particle size distribution variant

simulants.name: KIGAM-L1
simulants.institution: Korea Institute of Geoscience and Mineral Resources

simulants.name: KLS-1
simulants.institution: Korea Institute of Civil Engineering and Building Technology

simulants.name: Kohyama Simulant
simulants.notes: specialized simulant for water and oxygen extraction

simulants.name: LuNOR
simulants.notes:
A novel feature of this simulant is that it contains no
processed mineral concentrates but rather focuses on natural rock using resources
local to Norway

simulants.name: MLS-1
simulants.notes: High-Ti Mare derived from Duluth complex
simulants.particle_size_distribution: ground into a Particle Size Distribution (PSD) to resemble lunar soil

simulants.name: MLS-2
simulants.notes: highland simulant made of ground anorthosite from the Duluth Gabbro Complex, Minnesota

simulants.name: NEU-1
simulants.notes: Mix of basalt and volcanic slag

simulants.name: NU-LHT
simulants.notes: Part of NU-LHT series, includes NU-LHT-2M variant

simulants.name: NUW-LHT-5M
simulants.notes: Variant of NU-LHT with accurate glass

simulants.name: OPR Agglutinate
simulants.notes: Agglutinate component made out of any of the above simulants

simulants.name: OPRH4N
simulants.notes: Highland simulant that is a blend of basalt from the San Francisco volcanic field in Arizona and anorthosite from the Shawmere anorthosite complex in Canada. Designed to replicate the chemical, mineralogical, and geotechnical properties of lunar regolith. Agglutinates can be added (OPRH4W variant available).

simulants.name: TBG-1
simulants.notes: Best fit simulant to Apollo 14 soil R07 with distance d=10.00

simulants.name: TUBS-H
simulants.notes: Produced using a flexible preparation method where basalt is supplemented with minerals or sintered glass to achieve specific objectives

simulants.name: WHU-1
simulants.notes: Prepared based on particle size distribution data from samples returned by the Chang'e-5 mission

simulants.name: LMS-2
simulants.notes: Updated particle size up to 2 mm

simulants.name: LHS-2
simulants.notes: Updated particle size up to 2 mm

simulants.name: NU-LHT-4M
simulants.notes: Glass component offset by agglutinate glass

simulants.name: OPRFLCROSS1
simulants.notes: Volatile ice simulant based on LCROSS

simulants.name: TJ-2
simulants.particle_morphology: subprismoidal with subangular to angular shape

simulants.name: LZS-1
simulants.notes: Low-Ti mare basalt simulant from Peñas de Tao geosite, Lanzarote. Apollo 14 analogue.

simulants.name: LuSIC-1
simulants.notes: Lunar Simulant IBeA-Carabela. Sourced from 2021 Tajogaite volcano eruption lava (La Palma, Canary Islands). Developed using chemometrics and PCA comparison against Apollo 17 reference samples. Used by the Green Moon Project for a 2026 LEO plant-growth experiment.
